               The Secretary of Labor has determined that
               the publication of this periodical is necessary
               in the transaction of the public business re-
               quired by law of this Department. Use of funds
               for printing this periodical has been approved
               by the Director of the Office of Management
               and Budget through February 28, 1981. Con-
               trolled circulation postage paid at Riverdale,
               Md.

               Unless identified as copyrighted, articles and
               tabulations in this publication are in the public
               domain and may be reproduced without per-
               mission of the Federal Government. Please
               credit Employment and Earnings, Bureau of
               Labor Statistics.

               span. The indexes are calculated from 172 unpublished seasonally ad-         ESTIMATING METHODS
               justed employment series (two-digit nonmanufacturing industries and
               three-digit manufacturing industries) covering all nonagricultural             The principal features of the procedure used to estimate employ-
               payroll employment in the private sector. A more detailed discussion        ment for the establishment statistics are (1) the use of the "link
               of these indexes appears in "Introduction of Diffusion Indexes," in         relative" technique, which is a form of ratio estimation, (2) periodic
               the December 1974 issue of Employment and Earnings.                         adjustment of employment levels to new benchmarks, and (3) the use
                                                                                           of size and regional stratification.
               Labor turnover
                                                                                           The "link relative" technique
                   Labor turnover is the gross movement of wage and salary workers
               into and out of employed status with respect to individual
                                                                                              From a sample composed of establishments reporting for both the
               establishments. This movement, which relates to a calendar month, is
                                                                                           previous and current months, the ratio of current month employment
               divided into two broad types: Accessions (new hires and rehires) and
                                                                                           to that of the previous month is computed. This is called a "link
               separations (terminations of employment initiated by either employer
                                                                                           relative." The estimates of employment (all employees, including pro-
               or employee). Each type of action is cumulated for a calendar month
